notes: NI Abbey Road 50s Drummer — vintage 1950s drum kit recorded at Abbey Road Studios. Based on a period Ludwig/Gretsch-style kit with calf-skin heads. Minimal close-mic setup reflecting 50s recording practice — overhead and room mics dominate the sound. Character: warm, open, live room sound with natural decay; less separation than modern kits. Kick is soft and round (no felt-strip muting), snare is fat and papery. Suits jazz, early rock'n'roll, blues, and vintage-feel productions. Room mic is particularly important for this era's sound — push QC4 higher than you would on modern kits. The QC layout is identical across all Abbey Road Drummer instruments.

Quick Control 1.description: Kick Close Mic Level — soft and round 50s kick character; less sub than modern kits

Quick Control 2.description: Snare Top Mic Level — fat, papery 50s snare crack

Quick Control 3.description: Overhead Mic Level — period-accurate overhead capture; carries most of the kit's character

Quick Control 4.description: Room Mic Level — Abbey Road room ambience; push this higher than modern kits for authentic 50s open room sound

Quick Control 5.description: Kick Sub Level — low-end body of the kick (subtler on this kit than modern)

Quick Control 6.description: Snare Bottom Mic — snare wire character

Quick Control 8.description: Reverb/Room Blend — overall close vs room ratio

Overhead Level.description: Overhead mics — primary kit capture for this era

Room Level.description: Room microphone level — the defining characteristic of 50s drum sound

Compression.description: Bus compression — keep light for vintage authenticity
